entries.text: Go back to my slides.
Okay, so there are four different profiles, the analytical profile of possible audience, people that probably want data, logic and clear structure.

So it's, you know, this kind of approach that your audience might have. If you have this kind of profile, if your opponent is this kind of person, you better start with evidence and numbers and facts.

If your audience is a relational profile, so the most important thing for them is to feel heard and trusted, you'd better start with empathy and share goals. You know, using the kind of phrases that I mentioned before, like
if we are doing this collaboration project together, blah, blah, blah, we are going to earn something. We are going to work together as a team. We will succeed in this, this and this. Okay.
The third kind of profile is the expressive one.
These kind of people respond to vision.

The vision, so in this case I want to explain the term because maybe not all of you know what is the vision in terms of work and communication. The mission in terms again of working something, it's something that you are working on

right now. So you are currently, you currently have one, two, three goals that you want to achieve. The vision is about the future goals, it's about your ambitions. It's about something that might be the future result,

of your strategy, of your work, of whatever you're doing. That is very important to know. It's very important to know how to communicate it clearly. In negotiation as well might be very useful. But once again, it depends on your...
audience because the audience is a more practical one, they want to know the current goals.
